types.name: Physical
types.text: The most common damage, caused by weapons and impacts.

types.name: Air
types.text: Caused by supernatural winds and sky monsters.

types.name: Dark
types.text: Caused by undead and life-destroying magic.

types.name: Earth
types.text: Caused by supernatural tremors and earth spirits.

types.name: Fire
types.text: Caused by flames, magma or intense heat.

types.name: Ice
types.text: Caused by very low temperatures and frost magic.

types.name: Light
types.text: Caused by pure and untainted spiritual energy.

types.name: Poison
types.text: Caused by poison, infections and pollution.

no_type_damage.text: Damage that has no type (such as from the Arcanum of the Sword) is unaffected by affinities.

affinities_note.text: Some creatures have a particular Affinity towards certain damage types, often due to their Species — for instance, undead creatures are Vulnerable to light damage.

affinities.name: Vulnerability
affinities.text: If a creature is Vulnerable towards a damage type, they will lose twice the normal amount of Hit Points.

affinities.name: Resistance
affinities.text: If a creature Resists a damage type, they will only lose half the normal amount of Hit Points.

affinities.name: Immunity
affinities.text: If a creature is Immune to a damage type, they will lose no Hit Points.

affinities.name: Absorption
affinities.text: If a creature Absorbs a damage type, they will lose no Hit Points and will instead recover an amount of Hit Points equal to the damage they suffered.

affinity_resolution.text: Some game effects may cause you to gain a variety of Affinities: if a character is both Vulnerable and Resistant to a specific type of damage, they are treated as having no Affinity towards it. Immunity, on the other hand, always supersedes both Resistance and Vulnerability; finally, Absorption supersedes all other Affinities.
affinity_resolution.vulnerable_and_resistant: If a character is both Vulnerable and Resistant to a specific type of damage, they are treated as having no Affinity towards it.
affinity_resolution.immunity_supersedes: Immunity always supersedes both Resistance and Vulnerability.

extra_damage.text: Some game effects let you deal "extra damage". These effects do not apply to attacks or spells that deal no damage, only to those which already deal damage.

changing_damage_types.text: There are a variety of game effects that change the type of damage dealt by a spell or weapon. When this happens, the most recent effect "overwrites" any previous changes — and when such an effect ends, damage will return to its previous type.

improvised_damage_note.text: The Game Master should use the improvised_damage table to determine the amount of damage suffered in situations such as falling from a bridge or being buried under debris; the damage type will vary depending on the circumstances. Sources of damage such as traps and hazards cannot kill a Player Character unless they choose to sacrifice themselves; this should be portrayed as a sudden stroke of luck or as a consequence of the character's heroic resolve.
